note: Answer key for a candidate-selection task. Feature flags are normalized from each theme's real upstream theme.toml/docs (raw manifests shipped under sources/theme_candidates/raw_theme_manifests/). Offline + frozen -> deterministic. The conflict: the use case needs THREE features together (multilingual + team_members + publications) and only ONE candidate has all three; the name-tempting and popularity-tempting candidates each miss a hard requirement.

use_case_hard_requirements.HR2_team_members: a team/people profile system for a multi-person lab
use_case_hard_requirements.HR3_publications: an academic publication / BibTeX listing system
use_case_hard_requirements.HR4_static_hostable_permissive: Hugo static site, free hosting, permissive (MIT/Apache) license

soft_preferences.SC1_events_talks: also having an events/talks/news system is a plus for a research group

per_candidate.note: only candidate satisfying all of HR1+HR2+HR3+HR4, plus the events/talks soft plus

per_candidate.trap: NAME TRAP: 'Academic' in the name, has publications + i18n, but it is a single-person CV/résumé — no team/members system for a multi-person lab.
POPULARITY TRAP: one of the most popular Hugo themes, multilingual + fast, but no members and no publications system.
documentation theme; i18n yes but no academic features

gold.uniqueness: It is the ONLY candidate that satisfies all hard requirements (multilingual + team_members + publications + permissive). No tie-break is needed.

key_facts.academic_cv_is_a_name_trap: must NOT be selected as the primary; it lacks a team/members system (single-person CV) even though it's 'Academic' and has publications
key_facts.papermod_is_a_popularity_trap: must NOT be selected; most popular but lacks members AND publications
key_facts.all_candidates_are_multilingual_and_MIT: so i18n and license do NOT discriminate — the discriminators are HR2 (team members) and HR3 (publications)

scoring_anchors.note: Every candidate is multilingual + MIT, so an executor that justifies its pick only by 'it supports Chinese' or 'it's open source' has not done the real comparison. The decisive evidence is that only research_group has BOTH a team/members system AND a publications system. Executors should cite the theme.toml feature lists as evidence.
